5. Scaling Social Media with Ai Avatars with Jelani.mp4 So what I'm going to do is I'm going to show you my AI avatar workflow and I'm going to show you how I use AI as well in some interesting ways that you probably never really thought of and also just some tips and tricks. So let's get it. So what is an AI avatar? So, like Jeremy kind of said, it's a digital representation of you. So it could be a photo, it could be a video. As you can see here, I literally just replaced my face on some guy from like the 1960s in the war. But essentially, it mimics a person's likeness right and it allows technology— it allows businesses to really scale, so not only social media, but you have to think about like courses and webinars and onboarding flows, like there's a whole bunch of things that you can do outside of just social media. I'm going to talk about some of those things, but it is interesting and the one thing to know is like this is new technology, but it's rapidly rapidly getting better. Like from like six months ago, the voices, the lip movement, the hand movement, the head movement— I'm a little scared, but we'll see what happens. So real quick, I want to see if you guys can tell which one of these are AI. Let's see if you guys can tell. Imagine if you could import every single thing you've ever learned and clone yourself. I started those pages from scratch and again, like I started from zero and I was the real version of me and I just got sick of fucking filming content. I was like, 'I need to remove myself immediately.' Because what our business is, just for context, is we are an AI education company. So one of the main jobs is I have to go out and research tools. Going to be on the top of the game of what's out there. And a part of that is filming content, right? I don't use Google anymore. But I, so essentially, like, I use these tools in tandem to, by the way, all these images are AI also. Like, she's AI. But I use... these tools in a way that most people don't. So I'm going to share my workflow. But before I do, like I said, it took like 30 minutes to an hour, like a week or not even a week. Like every few days I had to write scripts. Right? And it got boring real quick. We run a database that has over 11,000 AI tools. So I have to be on top of all those tools. A lot of them are shit, but there are a lot that are really good. And it just takes a lot of time and I'm kind of lazy and I don't like doing that. So I have AI do it for me. So I replaced myself. The first thing I did was I figured out how to build a Chrome extension using ChatGPT, right? I'm sorry, a custom GBT, and I filled it out with examples. So that's the first thing I did. So essentially what I did was I built a script writer for me to write my scripts. That was the first way that I got... place myself. So essentially I had videos that, with the real version of me, I had a million views, two million views, 500k, whatever it was. I just took all those scripts. It's probably like 20 of them, and I had trained AI on how to essentially write those scripts. So now I just have to... I'll show you the process, but now I don't write scripts anymore. 85% there and I have what I call a AI content supervisor. It's a term I coined, but essentially it's someone who just looks at the content and makes sure it's pretty good because I don't trust AI 100% yet. And AI is really dumb, by the way. It's stupid as fuck. So the next thing I did was I built a Chrome extension, okay? So this is the part of the workflow that was really annoying was because... Had to research tools, I had to find out what the tool did, like did an overview, figure out the pricing, all this stuff, and it just took a lot of time. So I had AI build out a Chrome extension for me, so literally every time I find a cool tool, because I'm on newsletters, like Twitter, just random websites, I literally click a button and it sends it to my database. And then AI writes all that stuff out for me so I don't have to do that anymore and that saves me like 15 minutes every time I have to do a tool. Right. So I'm just like compounding my time, I'm getting my time time back, essentially. And this is a way that most people don't necessarily use AI, is like, if you think about a way that you can optimize your workflow, like think about about how you can build mini tools or custom GPTs that kind of take you out of the equation. So for this, I built a Chrome extension that just takes that part of my workflow out. Really simple for me and I don't have to worry about coming up with an overview. So you can see here I found this tool and this is what the Chrome extension does. It literally just sends it to my Airtable and then I have AI write all that stuff out for me. So then once it's in my Airtable, I don't have to do it. So you can see here, it writes all this stuff out for me from that link. What's that? Airtable is a database. So it's like Google Sheets. So that's the thing I'm going to talk about later. But essentially, it's like how you house all your... Information so for me, I house all the tools in there. I house my scripts in there. House all the different like the videos that the AI creates. So it's just like a database that's just pretty easy to use. I like it better over Google Sheets, but yeah. So the Chrome extension essentially does all this for me. So I don't have to do this anymore. So that's the first thing. Like, AI is really good at creating these little mini applications that you can create that will save you a lot of time. So instead of you know, hiring someone or spending time looking for the perfect tool, you can just think about it and tell AI to build it. It took me less than 40 minutes to build that and it saved me 15 minutes per tool every time. So, if you talk to AI and just say, 'Hey, like, literally,' I told it, 'I want to build a Chrome extension that sends the URL of the current page I'm on onto an Airtable database. Can you help me build this? Simple as that. And if you talk to it that way and you just kind of reiterate on what you want, you'll get the outcome that you want. So before we continue, before I show you the workflow, like I said, I don't trust AI yet, 100%. I really don't. It makes mistakes. It's really stupid. It's all about the input. So, if your input shits your outputs, gonna be shit. And that's like one thing most people don't realize is it's really dumb and it's just yeah, it's just dumb. So you have to be really good at understanding how to talk to it and I believe that you should have a hybrid approach. So rather than having AI do everything, it's like it's a mix between you and AI working together to come to a final outcome. Versus AI doing everything now. And then, once it's in Slack, which is your control center, again, you need a control center like my air table. So like, if this is yes, it's going to trigger that automation. So again, this is kind of like my central hub of information, right? As soon as it says yes, it goes to an API for Hagen. So if you're not technical, an API is essentially like a handshake between platforms where it's like, 'Okay, this person wants to create a video based off a certain amount of information, right? So Hagen allows us to automate this process via APIs. So you can see here that I tell it what I want as far as like the avatar ID, the script that I want it to use, and I mean that's pretty much it. So it's a little technical, but it's not that crazy. Because all I have to do is provide a script and then it's gonna create the video automatically for me and I don't have to really do anything besides let this run. So I have this automation. Once the video is done, it pulls it and it puts it back into Airtable. So, like, I have a file here so I can access this and, you know, view it. Not long ago, so it's accessed tonight. And also create a folder so that my editor gets notified. Like, this is all automated now. I don't have to do this anymore and it saves me a ton of time. Um, so that's why I have to edit because, like, you know, yeah, yeah, yeah, she cleans it up for sure, yeah. So when I first started using it, I could tell that it was AI because of the mouth, and it would be like a slight delay between what you're saying and the... So that's like a key tell. Like, if you've ever seen AI, you could tell the mouth... Is just a little weird, but in the recent months it's getting much better. So I would say that I would say in a few months you could probably just export and upload. But I think an editor right now needs to continue to kind of clean it up a bit. Then once that new voice model drops, I think it'll be like insane. But, yeah, that's it. So she, um... She basically just like cuts b-roll from the tool so she'll go to the tool website and she'll just record b-roll of like what the tool actually does or she'll go on YouTube and find the clips or whatever and then she just chops it up so like it's I do split: the bottom half is me or the AI avatar and the top is like the b-roll and that's all she does. So she just kind of puts visuals to what I'm saying as the AI avatar. The only thing she does to the voice, she adds a bit of dynamic compression, and then she also uses Adobe's Podcast Enhancer. I don't know if you ever use that. But she uses that, and then just added a bit of dynamic compression, and that's it. So it's nothing crazy. Thank you. So another opportunity that I don't think a lot of people are thinking about when it comes to this is translating content. So like entering new markets. So like, for example, this video I took, this is the AI version of me. And this is me speaking Spanish and I do not know how to speak Spanish. Hola, Jeremy, círculo interno soy yo hablando en español, pero literalmente no tengo idea de cómo hablar español. Loco, ¿verdad? Yeah. So imagine taking your content and translating it into Spanish or Japanese or Chinese or Portuguese. It's pretty crazy. So you can enter new markets with this and I don't think a lot of people are doing this on a mass scale. So that's another thing that you can definitely do. You do, but there's, I mean, you just find someone higher. Behavior is not the only tool. For example, LinkedIn. If you want to scale LinkedIn, there's AI avatars for LinkedIn automation. Where they will this company here they basically create a photo avatar of a person that's not real and you just create LinkedIn accounts with that avatar and then you can do outreach that way. There's also this other tool called Glado, which is pretty freaking sick, where you can scale your content creation using AI creators. So these creators basically rent out their likeness to this company, I believe, and you can essentially create content. Haijin also has something like this. Where you can do a similar thing, where essentially you enter a URL of a product, and then it will use an AI avatar as well. To put the person in front of the background. So I was on a call with them and they said the top industries right now are health and wellness and realtors. And I was like, 'That's it.' Interesting. Why are they doing that?
